prompt:
WIBTA if I took a bucket I paid for? So, I live with some people who have dogs, and I’ve got my own dog. Me and my dog are on a different floor of the house then everyone else. Anyway awhile back I wasn’t really going up there very often and when I was my dog didn’t always take time to drink. So I bought a water bucket just for him that would stay on that floor. The bucket upstairs was falling apart and all chewed up, so I paid a little extra for a better quality bucket, it’s meant to hold up to livestock so I figured it could handle a dog. Anyway about a year and a half ago, maybe longer, the dogs were outside and since my bucket was closer we brought that out for them all. But then for some reason I don’t remember it got brought upstairs, and the bucket that was falling apart got tossed. They asked if they could continue to use mine and I said, as long as they pay for me to get another. They never did, but it was fine because at that point I was spending more time up there and my dog was drinking more. Anytime I’ve mentioned it though they’ve offered for me to use the outside bucket that’s covered in algae and all chewed up. “Oh just clean it up and use it.” Imo they should use that because I paid for the better quality one. But I don’t say anything because it’d just cause unnecessary conflict. Anyway, here comes the part where I might be an asshole. I’m working on moving out, and I don’t have a water bucket for my dog. Especially with the recent string of dogs getting sick from algae, I’m not comfortable using that bucket even if it’s cleaned up. I’d like to take my original bucket with me, because otherwise I’d have to pay for another one, and I’d go for the same quality so again, paying a little bit more. My original bucket is still in great condition, so there’s no reason for me to get another one other then the fact that my housemates are using it for their dogs as well. So, WIBTA?

Edit, I know if I did take it they’d buy another one and not use the outside one, so their dogs would be safe and healthy, and not drinking out of the algae bucket.
response:
Taking back the water bucket you paid for is not wrong. You originally bought it for your dog, and your housemates have been using it without fulfilling their agreement to compensate you. Since you are moving out and need the bucket for your dog, it is reasonable to take it with you. 

Your housemates will need to purchase a new bucket for their dogs, which they should have done initially. This ensures their dogs' safety and health, as they will not have to rely on the algae-covered bucket. 

Here's what you should do: Inform your housemates that you will be taking the bucket when you move out, as it was your purchase and you need it for your dog. Be clear and direct about your decision, and remind them of the original agreement. This way, they can prepare to buy a new bucket for their dogs.
api_query: User asks if they would be wrong for taking back a water bucket they paid for. They originally bought the bucket for their dog, but housemates started using it for their dogs without compensating the user as agreed. User is moving out and wants to take the bucket, knowing housemates will buy another one for their dogs.
